scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein) — UNRELIABLE here: the register swap is masked to 0, so the sandbox cannot see the real gap. Full SHA1 is the only truth.

sessions.lever: diagnosis only — identified the two coupled cheats; no floor reduction (the real gap is masked)

reviewer.evidence: Two coupled forbidden cheats: (1) register pins result asm("s3")/sel2 asm("s2"); (2) regfix subst 'addiu $16,$zero,12'->'13' @149 (commented 'anti-cross-jump fix'). Removing pins flips RA (masked-invisible); removing the rule via setting case9/11 sel=0xD FAILS the full build. Likely needs cross-jump-defeating restructure + a pure-C RA lever, or canonical-asm auth.

remaining_gap.summary: 402-insn dispatcher. Target: result in $s3, sel2 in $s2, and a `li $s0,0xC`(case 10 load_sel2 path)/`li $s0,0xD`(case 9/11 sel_dispatch path) pair that the source's two `sel=0xC` statements + GCC jump2 cross_jump turn into a single shared constant, patched by the regfix to 13. Without pins GCC allocates result->$s2, sel2->$s3 (swap). The RA swap is invisible to the masked sandbox (scores stay 10), so only the full SHA1 reveals it.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD byte-matches ONLY via 2 cheat-asm pins + 1 regfix subst. No pure-C form found that yields target RA AND defeats the cross-jump merge.

next_hypotheses: RA: try block-local split / use-order levers (register-alloc-pure-c Lever A) to push sel2 into the lower $s2 and result into $s3 — sel2 dies earlier (its last use is func_8006BEC4(sel,sel2) mid-fn) while result lives to the return; the allocator gave result the lower reg, so shrink result's effective priority or extend sel2's. Verify ONLY via full build (retire), not the masked sandbox.

cross-jump: the regfix is an anti-cross-jump fix on the case9/11 vs case10 `sel=0xC`/`sel=0xD` tails reaching sel_dispatch. Apply the cross-jump-store-tail-merge lever family: make the two tails' ENDINGS not rtx_equal (mix goto-label vs inline forms) so jump2 won't merge, letting case9/11 emit its own constant. See .claude/rules/cross-jump-store-tail-merge.md / cross-jump-call-merge.md.
If both pure-C levers plateau, escalate for canonical-asm authorization or accept as a documented coupled plateau.

rejected_forms.reason: WRONG: full build SHA1 mismatch (tested via retire with pins kept). The regfix is an ANTI-CROSS-JUMP fix, not a value correction — case9/11 source value is genuinely 0xC; the 0xD at 80038ECC is produced by GCC's cross-jump behavior, not by a literal source 0xD. Do not re-derive.

Masked-sandbox 11->9 but that delta is the masked aligner reacting to the value, NOT a real RA fix — the s2/s3 swap persisted in the raw objdump. Not a real improvement; do not trust the masked delta here.
